parsed_review.summary: CP00-386 is a clean descriptor-only billing-core pack (RP12.P08.M02.S15-RP12.P08.M04.S12, 40 units, risk B) and its readiness claim is well-supported and appropriately scoped. Scope and all distributions (deliverable 20/16/2/2, micro-phase 6/22/12, micro-title) match across closeout-pack-plan.json, the review baseline, the registry binding, the contract projection, and the manifest. All 7 required exports exist; the descriptor/case-set/validator builders are pure constructors with no fs/network/exec/spawn calls, and freezeCp386Result/freezeCp386Validation stamp every output with the full no-write/no-real-data attestation. Three CP386 tests cover coverage counts, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries; the validator deep-equals contract<->registry for current_pack, cp386_requirements, cp386_no_write_attestation, p08_implementation_slice_descriptor, and historical bindings; command-evidence shows all pre-review commands at exit 0 with closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred to post-review. Boundaries hold: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, review status pending; Claude stays read-only and is not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false); Hermes is descriptor evidence only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false); review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence covers all CP178+ blockers and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. The pack does not overclaim production readiness or enterprise trust from local validation. One P3 (non-blocking) metadata note on changed-file-scope.json classification. No P0/P1/P2 issues.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies two untracked files as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-386/ appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145), which is contradictory by the field names' plain meaning. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 1717-1725) correctly excludes both, treating the cp00-386 docs dir as pack-owned (it is the pack's own manifest+command-evidence). new-session-handoff.md is a generic onboarding doc (references stale CP00-082/083 cursor from 2026-06-08) and is not referenced by any CP00-386 deliverable, validator, contract, or test, so it is genuinely unrelated to the pack content. This is a cosmetic metadata classification inconsistency only: active_pack_implementation_refs is correct, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (confirmed in validation-report.json), and full pack review was unaffected.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile the scope-generator so a file is not listed under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved; align changed-file-scope.json with the manifest's classification (pack-owned docs excluded from the unrelated list). No code or contract change needed for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_metadata_observation; does not affect pack scope, production_ready=false claim, or any no-write/read-only/Hermes boundary; CP00-386 closeout may proceed; recommend a follow-up tooling fix
